headline: Myanmar floor confirmation with bad-faith ceasefire notation. Junta extended earthquake ceasefire to May 31 while concurrently conducting nearly 300 attacks (NUG count) — UN Special Rapporteur Tom Andrews documented the junta firing on a Red Cross Society of China aid convoy in Sagaing. Two-month total: 550+ aerial and artillery attacks, hundreds of civilian deaths. Junta also denied visas, confiscated medicine, extorted aid workers, and blocked internet access. The ceasefire-while-attacking pattern is a distinct evidence type warranting BND notation under bad-faith governance even though composite remains at floor. Resolves pending 2026-04-30 proposal.
key_evidence: Junta extended earthquake ceasefire to May 31 (announced within window).
NUG documented nearly 300 civilian deaths in airstrikes during the junta's declared ceasefire periods. Two months post-quake: 550+ aerial and artillery attacks documented.
UN Special Rapporteur Tom Andrews confirmed: junta opened fire on a Red Cross Society of China convoy delivering aid in Sagaing.
Junta denied visas to international emergency response teams, confiscated medicine, extorted aid workers, blocked internet access, and harassed survivors.
Areas under opposition control — large portions of earthquake-affected regions — left 'largely devoid of external assistance' per internal UN report.
ICG 'Dangerous Drift' brief: regional detente signals weak; insufficient for floor-exit consideration.

evidence.finding: Junta extended ceasefire to May 31 with carve-outs allowing response to armed groups.
Junta bombs civilians during ceasefire, hits earthquake zones — extensively documented bad-faith pattern.
UN expert called for emergency SC action on ceasefire violations; documented junta firing on Chinese Red Cross convoy.
Year-on review of Myanmar earthquake response: junta exploited disaster to consolidate control; 3,800+ deaths, 5,000+ injured, 55,000+ homes destroyed.

scoring_rationale.AWR: 1 unchanged (floor). Earthquake response actively obstructed; suffering-detection infrastructure absent.
scoring_rationale.ACT: 1 unchanged (floor). Active harm continuation rather than response.
scoring_rationale.EQU: 1 unchanged (floor). Aid disproportionately blocked in opposition-controlled areas.
scoring_rationale.BND: 1 unchanged (floor). Bad-faith ceasefire pattern: declaring cessation while continuing attacks fits B3 Scope Clarity floor anchor at the strongest possible level. The pattern itself warrants notation but cannot move the score below floor.
scoring_rationale.INT: 1 unchanged (floor). I1 Consistency: declared commitments are systematically contradicted by simultaneous action — floor-level I2 Non-Performance sustained.

math_check.validator_check: Floor maintained. Floor-exit criteria not met.

bad_faith_ceasefire_notation: The pattern of declaring ceasefires (March 28, extended to May 31) while concurrently conducting ~300 documented attacks, denying aid, and firing on a Red Cross convoy is a distinct evidence type beyond ordinary armed conflict. Per methodology v1.2, this warrants explicit BND notation even though composite cannot move below the floor. Future cycles should treat ceasefire-extension events from this regime as inverted signals: each new ceasefire announcement that is concurrent with documented attacks is a negative integrity event, not a positive de-escalation event.
negativeSignals: Bad-faith ceasefire pattern documented by UN Special Rapporteur; 550+ attacks in two months post-quake; Red Cross convoy fired upon; aid systematically obstructed across visa, medicine, internet, and personnel dimensions; opposition-controlled areas left devoid of assistance.
positiveSignals: None identified. ICG 'Dangerous Drift' regional detente signals are weak — notation only.
sector_context: Floor-cluster countries (Myanmar, Sudan, South Sudan, Israel, North Korea, Eritrea) all confirmed at floor this cycle. Myanmar is distinguished by its bad-faith ceasefire pattern: simultaneously declaring and violating cessation agreements while obstructing humanitarian response. This pattern warrants distinct documentation across UN, HRW, and Fortify Rights sources.
open_actions_resolved: Pending 2026-04-30 proposal resolved with this assessment
Ceasefire-while-attacking pattern documented under BND notation
ICG regional detente notation only — insufficient for floor exit
open_actions_carried: Monitor May 31 ceasefire expiry — assess whether declaration extends or terminates
Monitor for any structural change in regional engagement (ASEAN, Russia, China)
Floor exit criteria require: cessation of documented attacks + restoration of humanitarian access + leadership accountability mechanism — none in trajectory
